input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS development)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)
- Install Xcode Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install`
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install additional components
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs list supported Xcode versions at [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/release-notes)

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest .NET 8+ SDK (LTS recommended)
- Or use Homebrew: `brew install dotnet-sdk`
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet --list-sdks` after install

## 3. Install the MAUI workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in Android SDK, iOS tooling, and MAUI templates.

## 4. Install Android dependencies

The MAUI workload installs the Android SDK automatically, but you may also need:

- **JDK 17** — bundled with the workload or install via `brew install openjdk@17`
- **Android emulator images** — manage via Android SDK Manager or:
  ```bash
  dotnet android sdk install --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
  ```

## 5. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit

- **Source:** [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com)
- Extensions: **C# Dev Kit**, **.NET MAUI** extension
- Alternatively, use **Visual Studio for Mac's successor** (VS Code is now the recommended macOS IDE)

---

## Discover the right versions

| Component | How to check |
|-----------|-------------|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --version` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload list` |
| Xcode | `xcodebuild -version` |
| Android SDK | `dotnet android sdk list` |
| JDK | `java --version` |

Check compatibility matrix at: **[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)**

---

## Verify everything works

```bash
# Create a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-ios --project TestApp.csproj

# Run on Android Emulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-android --project TestApp.csproj
```

If all four builds succeed and the app launches on a simulator/emulator, your environment is fully functional.
